title: [WIP] [CI:BUILD] Add Podman-in-Podman GitLab runner image
body:
***FIXME***: Add a real commit message.

***FIXME***: I'm not sure if the podman-repo is the appropriate place for this.

***FIXME***: This builds (including multi-arch) but is mostly untested as-is.  I am successfully running a previous version (privately) but that may just be my good-luck :smile: 

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign ashley-cui for approval by writing `/assign @ashley-cui` in a comment. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
force-push: Changed the `--privileged` default option to ***FALSE*** instead of true.  Add some other overrides as well, all else remains equal.

I'm going to abandon this for now in favor of https://gitlab.com/qontainers/pipglr because I cannot get our multiarch builds running.  It seems there aren't binaries available and/or they're named with oddball non-standard names.  Can re-open this and https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/233 if needs and/or situation changes.
